summary: Verified Semrush CSV upload + Screaming Frog Bridge backend endpoints and frontend Integrations UI for client e918ee9a-a135-4c5a-ae0b-f26f2f690117. All 13 new backend tests pass (after a small assertion relaxation — see minor issue below). Frontend SemrushUpload + SfBridge cards render with all data-testids; CSV upload flips per-type tile to populated (2 rows · 0s ago), trash icon clears it, SF Bridge save persists, status correctly surfaces ok=false / configured=true ('UNREACHABLE' badge), disconnect clears config, sf_bridge.py download serves text/x-python (8.3 KB) and README serves text/markdown.

minor.issue: When the configured base_url is unreachable (e.g. http://192.0.2.123:5005), response returns ok=false but `error` is an empty string. The except branch in sf_bridge.test_connection uses `str(e)[:200]` and httpx ConnectTimeout/ConnectError can stringify to ''. Frontend then renders the badge text fallback 'unreachable' (cosmetically OK), but the API contract is weaker than intended. Fix: in /app/backend/sf_bridge.py test_connection, set `err = str(e) or e.__class__.__name__` so the error field is always populated (e.g. 'ConnectTimeout', 'ConnectError').

action_items: (Minor) In /app/backend/sf_bridge.py::test_connection, fall back to the exception class name when str(e) is empty so /sf-bridge/status always returns a non-empty `error` for unreachable bridges.

(Optional) The keyword_research / competitor_analysis grounding via uploaded CSV was code-path verified (functions _build_semrush_keywords_block / _build_semrush_competitors_block / _build_semrush_gap_block consult uploads first and the workflow logs 'Grounded with Semrush ... (uploaded CSV)'). Live run was intentionally skipped per the testing brief to avoid API spend — main agent can self-verify by launching a small keyword_research run and inspecting run.logs.
critical_code_review_comments: semrush_csv.py + sf_bridge.py are clean, focused modules with proper async storage helpers.
/api/integrations/sf-bridge/status calls httpx with a 15s timeout on every load — fine for an internal single-user app but could be reduced to 5s to avoid sluggish UI when the bridge is offline.
SfBridge.jsx uses window.confirm() for disconnect — works but inconsistent with the rest of the app (Radix dialogs). Minor UX nit.
sf_bridge.test_connection swallows empty exception strings — see backend minor issue above.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seed client e918ee9a-a135-4c5a-ae0b-f26f2f690117 (Cooking Italians). Test uploads created during the run were cleaned up by the trailing test_zz_cleanup_remaining_semrush_uploads step and frontend disconnect.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_semrush_sf_bridge_api.py (live HTTP against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL). Frontend e2e: route /clients/{id}/integrations now contains SemrushUpload + SfBridge cards with full data-testid coverage (sf-bridge-card, sf-bridge-state, sf-bridge-url, sf-bridge-token, sf-bridge-save, sf-bridge-download, sf-bridge-readme, sf-bridge-disconnect, sf-bridge-refresh, semrush-upload-card, semrush-upload-btn, semrush-file-input, semrush-upload-{type}, semrush-clear-{type}). Disconnect uses window.confirm — Playwright must register a `page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept())` handler. /status takes up to ~15s when the bridge URL is unreachable due to httpx timeout, so allow ~20s after Save before asserting state.
rca of the issue: Minor backend: sf_bridge.test_connection's except branch returns `str(e)[:200]` which is '' for some httpx exceptions (e.g. ConnectTimeout). Reproduction: POST /api/clients/{id}/integrations/sf-bridge/configure {base_url: 'http://192.0.2.123:5005', token: 't'} then GET /api/clients/{id}/integrations/sf-bridge/status → response {configured:true, ok:false, error:''}. Mitigation: use `err = str(e) or type(e).__name__`.
